name: Texas Breakfast Tacos
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Green Bell Pepper (seeded and cut into strips)
10 Flour Tortillas (7 to 8 inches in diameter)
6 oz Cheese, Shredded (mixture of shredded Colby and Monterey Jack)
recipeInstructions:
1. Seed and cut the green bell pepper into strips.
2. Prepare the Southwest Guacamole and Fresh Tomato Salsa; reserve.
3. Cook the chorizo sausage, onion, and bell pepper strips in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat, stirring frequently, until the sausage is done (about 10 minutes). Drain and reserve.
4. Heat 1 tbsp margarine in a skillet over medium heat until hot and bubbly.
5. Pour beaten eggs into the hot skillet.
6. As the eggs begin to set at the bottom and sides, gently lift cooked portions with a spatula so that the thin uncooked portion can flow to the bottom. Avoid constant stirring.
7. Cook until the eggs are thickened throughout but still moist (about 5 minutes).
8. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F.
9. Spoon about 1/4 cup sausage mixture onto each tortilla. Top with about 1/4 cup of eggs and 2 tbsp of cheese mixture.
10. Fold tortillas into halves.
11. Arrange 5 assembled tacos in an ungreased jelly roll pan (15 ½ x 10 ½ x 1-inch). Brush with melted margarine.
12. Bake until light golden brown, about 10 to 12 minutes. Repeat with the remaining tacos.
13. Serve with guacamole and salsa.
